task_completion.description: Verify the agent accesses the bologna_acosta SUMO scenario.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the agent navigating to /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_acosta/ or opening the scenario files in a text editor, terminal, or SUMO-GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent configures the Surrogate Safety Measures (SSM) output with the correct thresholds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of a configuration file (.sumocfg), Python script (TraCI), or terminal command showing the device.ssm.probability, device.ssm.file set to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/ssm_output.xml, TTC threshold (3.0), and DRAC threshold (3.5).

task_completion.description: Verify the agent runs the SUMO simulation to generate the SSM XML data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing SUMO executing (either SUMO-GUI running the network or terminal output showing simulation steps progressing) and the creation of ssm_output.xml.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent produces the intersection safety report in CSV format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a script generating or a viewer/editor displaying intersection_safety_report.csv in the /home/ga/SUMO_Output/ directory, containing parsed safety data.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent produces the safety audit summary text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a script generating or a text editor displaying safety_audit_summary.txt in the /home/ga/SUMO_Output/ directory, containing summary statistics referencing the critical limits (TTC < 1.5s, DRAC > 5.0).

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually executed the SUMO simulation rather than mocking the XML output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal logs showing SUMO loading the network and routing, or the SUMO-GUI actively running, rather than a script that just writes a hardcoded XML file.

integrity.description: Verify the CSV and TXT reports contain genuine processed data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a script (e.g., Python) that parses the ssm_output.xml to calculate the results, or check that the CSV/TXT files contain realistic, varied numerical data rather than placeholder or copy-pasted text.

integrity.description: Verify the output files are actually saved to the requested directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal commands (like 'ls /home/ga/SUMO_Output/') or a file manager window confirming the files exist in the exact specified paths, not just printed to standard output.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Scenario: bologna_acosta. Expected output paths: /home/ga/SUMO_Output/ssm_output.xml, /home/ga/SUMO_Output/intersection_safety_report.csv, /home/ga/SUMO_Output/safety_audit_summary.txt. Required thresholds: TTC <= 3.0s, DRAC >= 3.5 m/s^2. Critical limits: TTC < 1.5s, DRAC > 5.0 m/s^2.
